vulnerability.reason: Reference highlights homogeneous agent architecture as the sensitivity, while generated focuses on missing safeguards and unconditional memory sharing; these are related but distinct conditions enabling propagation.
vulnerability.reference: Homogeneous agent architecture enables rapid propagation of identical jailbreak exploits.
vulnerability.generated: Agents lack safeguards to filter adversarial images and unconditionally share multimodal memories, enabling unchecked propagation of jailbreak triggers.

impact.reason: Both reference and generated describe all agents being corrupted and generating harmful outputs across the system; generated also includes user safety and integrity compromise, which aligns with the damage type.
impact.reference: All agents corrupted, generating harmful outputs across the entire system.
impact.generated: Exponential jailbreak of up to a million agents generates mass harmful content, compromising safety for all users and undermining system integrity.

dose_response.reason: The causal arc from adversarial images spreading jailbreak prompts via multi-agent chat to infecting agents and generating harmful content is semantically present, with additional detail on memory sharing still mapping to the same infectious propagation logic.
dose_response.reference: Infectious propagation of jailbreak prompts via adversarial images in multi-agent chats corrupts all agents, leading to systemic harmful outputs and user exposure to toxic content.
dose_response.generated: Propagation of jailbreak prompts via adversarial images in multi-agent chat, through message exchange and user outputs, triggers unfiltered memory sharing, leading to exponential jailbreak infecting up to a million agents, generating harmful content and compromising user safety and system integrity.
